audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Cana sign, mother of Jesus, water into wine, first sign/glory, temple action, temple/body saying, and trust/knowing motif.

verses.restored: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
Jesus also was invited to the wedding, and his disciples.
When the wine ran short, the mother of Jesus says to him, They have no wine.
Jesus says to her, What to me and to you, woman? My hour has not yet come.
His mother says to the servants, Whatever he says to you, do.
There were six stone water jars set there according to the purification of the Judeans, each holding two or three measures.
Jesus says to them, Fill the jars with water. They filled them up to the top.
He says to them, Draw now and carry to the steward of the feast. They carried it.
When the steward of the feast tasted the water that had become wine, and did not know from where it was, though the servants who had drawn the water knew, the steward calls the bridegroom,
and says to him, Every person sets out the beautiful/good wine first, and when they have drunk freely, the lesser; you have kept the beautiful/good wine until now.
This beginning of signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples trusted into him.
After this he went down to Capernaum, he and his mother, brothers, and disciples, and they remained there not many days.
The Passover of the Judeans was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
He found in the temple those selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and the money-changers seated.
Making a whip from cords, he drove all out of the temple, both sheep and cattle; he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned the tables.
To those selling doves he said, Take these things from here; do not make my Father's house a house of marketplace/trade.
His disciples remembered that it is written, Zeal for your house will consume me.
Therefore the Judeans answered and said to him, What sign do you show us, since you do these things?
Jesus answered and said to them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it.
The Judeans said, This temple was built in forty-six years, and you will raise it in three days?
But he was speaking about the temple of his body.
When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they trusted the Scripture and the word Jesus had spoken.
While he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many trusted into his name, beholding the signs he was doing.
But Jesus himself was not entrusting himself to them, because he knew all people,
and because he had no need that anyone should bear witness concerning a human, for he himself knew what was in the human.
